_about.title: Woodpecker backfill schema — everything our DB stores/reads
_about.purpose: The canonical field set required to backfill a tender into Woodpecker's serving layer, grounded in what compile.py emits and the app/search/stats actually read. Organised by SERVING ARTIFACT (index entry, record, release, observation, status, stats), not by source. RTI is one sourceRoute among several (live | ocp-bulk | rti | attended-capture); the RTI-askable subset + legal notes live in `_import/rti_application.md` and the `sourceRoutes`/`contestability` blocks here.

fillLegend.source: mapped from the source data (RTI reply / OCDS export / portal) by the importer/scraper
fillLegend.derived: computed by compile.py (or the bulk importer) from other fields — do not supply by hand
fillLegend.observed-only: produced ONLY by Woodpecker's live fetch (witness: woodpecker). Declared/imported backfills CANNOT fill it — default it (false / 0 / null).
fillLegend.constant: fixed for an import (e.g. imported:true, currency INR)

statusLegend.partial: partially populated (e.g. a count, not the records)
statusLegend.reserved-empty: schema slot/tag exists, nothing fills it
statusLegend.new: not captured by any route, and/or needs a code change to carry

perTenderReleases.how: Write OCDS releases[] (+ observations[]) to the ledger; compile.py derives the record, compiledRelease, index entry and stats automatically.
usedBy: any per-tender enrichment incl. attended AOC capture

bulkImport.how: Write the index shards + status.json DIRECTLY (engine:import). compile.py detects engine==import and does NOT recompile (it would clobber). No per-tender record/release/observation files are produced.
derives: stats (entries are folded into corpus totals — declared, add 0 to silent-edit/pull tallies)
bulkImport.appNote: For imported sources the app renders the detail view straight from the index entry — there is no record file. So for the bulk path the index entry IS the whole backfilled record; anything the detail view should show must be in it.

indexEntry._note: The DB ROW. Read by the list view, stats, and search-meta. For the bulk path the importer writes this directly; for the per-tender path compile.py derives it. Fields below marked fill:observed-only are defaulted (false/0) for declared imports.

fields.name: ocid
fields.note: Primary key. Stable across the tender's life.

fields.name: buyer
fields.path: buyer.name (string, not object, in the index)
fields.note: Organisation chain, flattened to a string here.

fields.name: value
fields.note: ESTIMATE / put-to-tender value. Drives value-band stats. {} if unknown.

fields.name: publishedDate
fields.note: Year/month shard key. Needed for the Stats scrubber.

fields.name: latestTag
fields.note: tender | tenderAmendment | tenderUpdate | award. For bulk imports set to the latest stage you actually have.

fields.name: lastReleaseDate
fields.note: Sort key; = publishedDate for a single-release import.

fields.name: corrigendumCount
fields.note: Count of tenderAmendment releases. Today only a count is carried, not the amendment content.

fields.name: hasDeadLink
fields.note: A document with sourceAlive:false but a stored url. false for a fresh import.

fields.name: hasOcr
fields.note: Any document extracted via OCR. false unless you OCR RTI PDFs.

fields.name: hasUndeclaredChange
fields.note: Silent-edit flag. Declared imports default FALSE (no capture-floor).

fields.name: procurementMethod
fields.note: open | limited | EOI | single | global. Assam import carried it; live does not.

awardFieldsToAdd._note: NET-NEW index fields required to SURFACE award data in the list/stats. compile.py index_entry() does NOT carry these today — adding them is a code change in index_entry() + the bulk importer + the app's list/detail render + (optionally) build_stats. Without these, RTI award data lives only in the release objects and never shows in the DB views.

fields.name: supplierName
fields.note: The winner. Settled-disclosable for a concluded contract.

fields.name: awardedValue
fields.note: Compare vs value (estimate) = the estimate->award delta; the headline metric.

fields.name: numberOfTenderers
fields.note: Bid count. 1 = the red-flag signal. Highest-value single new field.

release._note: OCDS release for the per-tender path (compile.py folds these into the record + derives the index entry). Shape from ocds.build_release + build_tender.

subfields.name: sourceUrl
subfields.note: original portal url; retained even after it 404s

fields.name: amendments
fields.note: build_release accepts amendment objects; nothing fills them. subfields: date, description, rationale, changes[]

fields.name: awards
fields.note: THE GAP. subfields: id, date, status, value{amount,currency}, suppliers[]{name,id}, rationale

fields.name: contracts
fields.note: extension beyond v0.1 subset. subfields: id, dateSigned, value{amount,currency}, period{startDate,endDate,durationInDays}, status, implementation{transactions[], milestones[]}

record._note: compile.py output read by the detail/lifecycle view (per-tender path only; imports have no record). All DERIVED — listed so you know the read shape, not to backfill by hand.

observation._note: NOT BACKFILLABLE from declared sources. Produced only by Woodpecker's own live fetch. RTI/exports leave this empty; their index rows default the observed-only fields. (An RTI figure may appear as a witness:'rti' corroboration record in v1.2 — that is NOT a backfill of this timeline.)

status._note: Source-level health + provenance. REQUIRED for every source (a pill renders from it; compile folds engine:import sources via it).

fields.name: engine
fields.note: 'import' for RTI/bulk (so compile won't recompile); 'gepnic' for live

fields.name: provenance
fields.note: see provenanceRecord below — the honesty contract

stats._note: Corpus aggregates (build_stats). Fully DERIVED from the index entries above — listed so you know which entry fields drive which aggregate; nothing to backfill directly. Adding award fields to indexEntry means extending build_stats to aggregate them (e.g. award-value totals, estimate->award delta, bid-count bands).

provenanceRecord._note: Stamp onto an RTI/bulk source's status.json. Mirrors assam_archive PROVENANCE. Keeps declared data honestly separated from observed capture.
provenanceRecord.source: RTI disclosure — <department>, reply dated <YYYY-MM-DD>, RTI ref <no.>
provenanceRecord.license: <as stated in the reply; often none>
provenanceRecord.note: Declared official disclosure — NOT observed by Woodpecker (no capture-floor snapshots).

witness._note: Optional v1.2 role: an RTI figure that DISAGREES with the portal's declared figure is a cross-witness contradiction. Add 'rti' to the enum. Distinct from backfill — this corroborates, it does not fill the observation timeline.

live.covers: notice-stage, forward-only; re-polls known tenders -> observations. No bulk history; AOC only per-tender via attended capture.

ocp-bulk.covers: notice-stage bulk from data.gov.in / OCP registry. EXHAUSTED at Assam + Himachal.

rti.covers: notice backfill for the ~31 non-OCP states + the BULK award/contract stage + RTI-obtained PDFs. The only bulk route to the award tag.

attended-capture.covers: specific completed/awarded tenders by id/keyword past the CAPTCHA. Per-tender enrichment incl. AOC outcomes; NOT bulk.

disclosable.basis: Winner, awarded/contract value, bid count and dates of a CONCLUDED public contract are routinely held disclosable in the public interest.

contestable.strategy: Ask non-contestable award/contract/count fields first; bidder-level financials in a separable paragraph, so a Section 10 severance still yields the core.
